title: Supply Chain, Deployment, Migration, Backup, and Recovery · Evidence Triage
summary: A preserved decision record for supply chain, deployment, migration, backup, and recovery contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in release integrity and clean recovery proof and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.
Bind protected source and workflow, isolated build, declared inputs, immutable output, SBOM, provenance, signature or attestation, and verification.
Move one reviewed artifact through compatible migration, readiness, bounded exposure, guardrails, hold, abort, promotion, and user communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Supply Chain Inventory
steps.instruction:
debug case — A mutable release has unknown components, weak builder identity, risky migration, successful backup status, and no isolated application-level restore proof. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.

Inspect the Supply Chain Inventory trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Supply Chain Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Supply Chain Inventory, keep Supply Chain Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Supply Chain Inventory, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Supply Chain Inventory, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Supply Chain Inventory precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Supply Chain Inventory, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A mutable release has unknown components, weak builder identity, risky migration, successful backup status, and no isolated application-level restore proof. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Supply Chain Inventory, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A dependency scanner inventories the whole product.

stimulus.title: Supply Chain Inventory case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A mutable release has unknown components, weak builder identity, risky migration, successful backup status, and no isolated application-level restore proof. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A dependency scanner inventories the whole product.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership. Artifact under review: release integrity and clean recovery proof.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Supply Chain Inventory without its stated preconditions, omit the release integrity and clean recovery proof trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Supply Chain Inventory for release integrity and clean recovery proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Supply Chain Inventory, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Supply Chain Inventory record says release integrity and clean recovery proof looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Supply Chain Inventory.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Retirement Plan
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Retirement Plan: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A mutable release has unknown components, weak builder identity, risky migration, successful backup status, and no isolated application-level restore proof. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Retirement Plan, keep Retirement Plan connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.
